summary: Context7 MCP config failed with 'url is not supported for stdio'.
root_cause: Context7 server block mixed STDIO fields with HTTP URL fields.
resolution: Use STDIO-only Context7 config with command='npx' and args=['-y','@upstash/context7-mcp']; do not mix url with command/args.
notes: User-level config outside repo was not edited because it may contain user secret material.

summary: Backend/frontend verification commands could not start because go and npm were not available in a previous default shell PATH.
root_cause: The earlier Codex shell environment reported 'command not found' for both go and npm.
resolution: Use the current Windows PowerShell workspace toolchain or activate the bundled/project toolchain before running checks; current workspace now resolves both go and npm.
notes: Resolved in the current workspace after toolchain availability was verified. No secrets or environment values were recorded.

summary: Go and npm checks may fail inside sandbox when they need home-directory build/module/cache/log paths.
root_cause: Managed sandbox blocks writes under the user home cache directories used by Go toolchain/module cache and npm cache/logs.
resolution: Retry verification commands with scoped escalation for `go test`, `go vet`, `npm ci` or localhost `curl` when the first failure is cache/write/bind related.
notes: Resolved after scoped sandbox escalation for verification commands. No secrets, env values, tokens or provider credentials were recorded.

summary: Cloudflare Dashboard-managed Mini App tunnel failed while VPN or local network filtering blocked Cloudflare edge connectivity; after VPN was disabled the connector registered, but the public hostname still returned 502 due to edge resets or stale connectors.
root_cause: Outbound cloudflared connectivity to Cloudflare edge on port 7844 was blocked or reset by VPN, firewall, antivirus, ISP routing or a stale cloudflared service/process. Multiple connectors for the same tunnel can leave Cloudflare routing to a broken local connector.
resolution: Disable VPN, stop all cloudflared processes/services, run exactly one dashboard-managed Mini App connector with protocol http2, verify local Vite/API first, then verify https://app.neiirohub.ru. If edge resets remain, test from another network or run the connector on another machine/server.
checks: Cloudflare remote ingress was app.neiirohub.ru -> http://127.0.0.1:5173
notes: Do not store or print tunnel tokens. Runtime logs/process args may have exposed a token before launcher cleanup; patched launcher avoids token command-line args and does not pass CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TOKEN to child cloudflared.

summary: Public /metrics probe returned Prometheus exposition from vk.neiirohub.ru while app.neiirohub.ru returned only HTML SPA fallback.
root_cause: A Cloudflare tunnel ingress route forwarded the public hostname to a local metrics-capable origin without a higher-priority /metrics deny rule. App-level private metrics guards can be bypassed when the tunnel/origin path hides public host or client headers from the Go process.
resolution: Add a higher-priority Cloudflare ingress/Public Hostname rule for the affected hostname and path ^/metrics(?:$|/) with service http_status:404, restart the active connector, keep Go /metrics guarded against public forwarded headers, and make the blackbox public metrics probe match Prometheus body content rather than plain HTTP 2xx.
checks: public HEAD showed vk.neiirohub.ru/metrics returned Prometheus text/plain
local app guard rejected Cloudflare-like forwarded headers with 404
blackbox public metrics probe now ignores HTML fallback and still detects real Prometheus exposition
notes: No metrics body, tunnel token, process command line, raw headers, secrets or user data were recorded.

summary: Provider output download failures could persist full remote output URLs into job errors, traces or DLQ records.
root_cause: artifactservice wrapped downloader failures with the original remote URL before returning the error to the worker failure path.
resolution: Redact URL-like substrings before recording or returning remote artifact download errors; keep only a generic download failure message and redaction marker.
checks: unit test asserts signed/private URL parts do not appear in SaveRemoteArtifact errors
notes: No provider URL, query string, prompt, artifact body or credential was recorded.

summary: When mock was present in the provider chain, the artifact downloader used a mock-aware HTTP fallback without SSRF protections.
root_cause: cmd/worker replaced the default artifactservice downloader with mock.NewDownloader, whose non-mock HTTP branch used the default HTTP client directly.
resolution: Make mock downloader handle only synthetic mock/data URLs and delegate real provider URLs to artifactservice's SSRF-hardened HTTP downloader.
checks: unit tests cover data URL handling, fallback delegation and rejection without fallback
notes: No target URL body, provider payload or credentials were recorded.

summary: Dev Postgres, Redis and MinIO ports were published on all interfaces with development credentials.
root_cause: Base docker-compose.yml used host port mappings without a 127.0.0.1 bind address.
resolution: Bind dev service ports to 127.0.0.1 so they remain reachable from local tools but are not exposed on LAN/VPN interfaces by default.
checks: docker compose config validates the resulting port bindings
notes: No database content, passwords or object data were recorded.

summary: Production configuration could still accept mock provider or mock payment settings and unknown provider-chain entries could be skipped later by worker startup.
root_cause: Config validation checked image/video/payment provider names but did not validate PROVIDER/PROVIDER_CHAIN or forbid mock providers in production.
resolution: Validate PROVIDER and each PROVIDER_CHAIN entry, and fail closed when production config contains mock generation or payment providers.
checks: unit tests cover unknown provider-chain entries and mock provider/payment rejection in production
notes: No environment values or secrets were recorded.

summary: A runtime-local Cloudflare tunnel config under .runtime was tracked even though .runtime is ignored.
root_cause: The file was added before or despite the ignore rule, so local tunnel metadata such as credentials-file paths remained in Git history/worktree diffs.
resolution: Remove the runtime config from Git tracking while keeping the local file ignored and generated by setup scripts.
checks: git status shows the file as a staged deletion rather than a modified tracked runtime config
notes: No credential file contents, tunnel token or Cloudflare secret was recorded.

summary: Go targeted tests failed in the managed sandbox when the default build cache under the user AppData directory was inaccessible.
root_cause: The test command tried to open compiled cache artifacts under a non-workspace GOCACHE path, and the sandbox denied access.
resolution: Run Go checks with GOCACHE set to a workspace-local path such as D:\vk_agregator\.cache\go-build.
checks: go test targeted packages passed with workspace-local GOCACHE
notes: No source code, secrets, prompts, launch params or environment values were recorded.

summary: Mini App Playwright smoke reported all tests passed but the npm command did not exit when Playwright owned the Vite webServer on Windows.
root_cause: The integrated Playwright webServer lifecycle could leave the command waiting after tests completed in the local Windows shell.
resolution: Run the npm smoke script through a small Node runner that starts Vite directly, runs Playwright with the config webServer disabled, and terminates the owned Vite process in finally.
checks: npm --prefix web/miniapp run e2e:smoke passed and exited with code 0
notes: No secrets, prompts, full launch params, raw payloads, private URLs or PII were recorded.
